title: scaevola subcapitata
intro: Scaevola subcapitata, also known as the Blue Fan Flower, is a flowering plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Goodeniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Scaevola subcapitata is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its herbal properties to be utilized for medicinal purposes.
